compiled_state.title: Write RED tests for hook_record_agent_attribution
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Create tests/hooks/test-post-functions-attribution.sh with two failing test functions: test_hook_record_agent_attribution_writes_jsonl_entry_when_enabled() and test_hook_record_agent_attribution_skips_write_when_disabled(). Tests must run in fresh subshells due to _POST_FUNCTIONS_LOADED=1 guard. Commit only the test file; CI fails (RED state — intended). TDD exemption: test-writing task.

comments.body:
Gap Analysis AC amendment: read_config_value mechanism must be specified in tests.

T1a tests assert that hook_record_agent_attribution uses read_config_value to check attribution.enabled. The function read_config_value does not exist as a standalone function in this codebase — the existing pattern is to call bash "$SCRIPTS_DIR/read-config.sh" <key>.

Amended AC1/AC2: The test setup must use a PATH-prepend mock for read-config.sh (returning 'true' or empty) rather than mocking a nonexistent read_config_value function. Test setup should:
  - Create a temporary mock read-config.sh in a temp bin dir that returns 'true' when called with 'attribution.enabled', or returns empty string for AC2
  - Prepend that temp bin dir to PATH before sourcing/calling hook_record_agent_attribution
  - Verify JSONL output based on which mock response is active

This ensures the RED test fails for the right reason (function absent), not because of a config mechanism mismatch.
comments.author: Test
